name: The Meter
oneLineEssence: The reading is the hero: one fat tabular-figure value sitting against its expected band so "expect 12V — pump silent? suspect ground" reads as a verdict at a glance, with the lift-pump circuit as the quiet backdrop that proves WHERE that number comes from.
feltExperiencePitch: You're under a 2018 F-250, key on, back-probing the lift pump's power wire. You open Vyntechs and the screen is almost entirely the diagram — but only the lift-pump corner of it is lit: the Lift Pump Relay, the 12V wire running to the Electric Lift Pump, its ground to chassis, and off in the corner the Fuel Rail Pressure sensor glowing faintly because that's the symptom that sent you here. The other twenty parts of the fuel system are still drawn, just dimmed back like the rest of the truck went quiet. You tap the pump's 12V pin. A reading rises up from the bottom edge — big, like a meter face: "EXPECT 12V" sitting right above "key-on prime, then 0V," and under it, plain, "0V when commanded? Go upstream — relay, fuse, pump driver." You don't read a paragraph; you SEE the number you should get and the one thing to do if it's wrong. Tiny grey type under the value says "inferred from theory" — the tool is telling you it drafted this from how the system works, not from a verified shop manual, and if you want to know why it believes that, you tap those two words and it tells you, then folds back away. Nothing covers the pin you're probing; nothing nags you about steps; the failure tree only unfolds the moment your reading is actually wrong. It feels less like a web page and more like a scope that already knows what good looks like.

focusFadeMechanic: Focus is organized around the active READING, not the active region — this is the thesis. Each walk step emits a `focusPinId` (the pin under test, e.g. the Lift Pump 12V Power pin), the step's `circuitNodeIds` (Lift Pump Relay, Electric Lift Pump, the 12V wire + ground edges, plus the FRP Sensor as the downstream symptom anchor), and a step `type` (locate | probe | interpret | branch). Implementation against @xyflow/react: (1) NODES — extend the existing `toFlowElements`/`TopologyNode` to take a `tier` per node: `focus` (in circuitNodeIds and on the active reading's path), `anchor` (the FRP sensor — the one leading bit of "why we're here" context, drawn at ~0.45), or `recede` (everything else → `--vt-recede: 0.16`, the value already baked into `.wire.off`). Tier drives a class, not display:none — the CP4.2 pump, all 8 injectors, FRP regulator, IMV, PCM stay drawn as the whole-truck anchor. (2) WIRES — reuse the EXISTING `.wire.is-active` (3.5px + `drop-shadow(0 0 6px currentColor)`) for the focus circuit and `.wire.off` (0.16, `animation:none`) for receded wires; the only change is the DRIVER — today it's pin-selection, it must become the step. The 12V wire to the lift pump keeps its `steady-12v` dash-flow ONLY when it's in the focus tier; receded wires freeze (already true for `.wire.off`). (3) CAMERA — modest or no move, deliberately: because The Meter bets on the reading not the region, the camera does a GENTLE `reactFlowInstance.fitView({ nodes: circuitNodeIds.map(id=>({id})), padding: 0.28, maxZoom: 1.1, duration: 320, interpolate:'smooth' })` only on step-change, never the aggressive zoom The Drive would use — the reframe is a settle, not a flight. The reading rising into the bottom band is the louder event. (4) PERSISTENCE — the fade re-resolves only on step-advance (Altium persistent-mask rule); within a step, tapping parts never re-fades and never moves the camera. A non-zero opacity floor is enforced so "receded" never becomes "vanished."
tapToPullBehavior: A tap is the tech declaring "tell me about this part," and the answer rises from the BOTTOM edge as a canvas-internal sheet — "The Meter" — never a right-rail document, never a popover pinned to the moving node (Figma-UI3 cautionary). The fitView padding is asymmetric and bottom-heavy (`{top:0.16, right:0.24, bottom:0.40, left:0.20}` desktop) so the lift-pump cluster already sits ABOVE where the sheet lands — the pump the tech is probing is never covered (spine rule 5). Desktop: the sheet occupies the bottom band (~30% height, full width minus the ribbon), anchored to a fixed edge with a predictable home. Its content is led by the READING for THIS pin in THIS scenario: a label row ("LIFT PUMP · 12V POWER"), then the instrument block — `EXPECT 12V` rendered in `.vt-mono-big` (32px tnum) inside a quiet expected-band frame, with the live `scenario.pinReadings[pinId]` ("12V briefly during prime, then 0V") set directly beside it and a single in-range / low chip. Below that, dimmed to ~0.55 and one logical level down (iA focus-mode): connector, # wires/pins + what each does ("12V power, ground to chassis"), location ("driver-side frame rail near tank"), and the short Operational Theori (drawn from `component.role`: "Pulls fuel from the tank filter, pushes it to the engine filter and on to the CP4 — target ~55 psi at idle"). The failure tree (`pin.missingLogic`) stays COLLAPSED on a passing/in-range reading and only unfolds — animated, in-place — when the reading is actually out of range (expertise-reversal: never pre-load the failure tree onto a good test). Dismiss on background-tap (existing `onPaneClick`/`onClearSelection`) or the close affordance; the sheet eases down, the receded harness holds (camera does NOT move on dismiss). Tapping a different pin within the same step swaps the sheet contents with a quick cross-fade — no camera move, no re-fade.
stepAwareOrdering: The same tap on the same Lift Pump 12V pin is ordered differently by the moment, driven by step `type` — this per-step-TYPE budget is what makes The Meter distinct from a uniform highlight rule. PROBE/INTERPRET step ("back-probe the lift pump power feed, key-on" — the representative scene): lead with WHERE to put the probe (`pin.probeLocation`: "back-probe the power wire at the lift pump connector under the truck near the tank") immediately followed by the instrument block EXPECT 12V vs NOW, plus the verdict; connector / # pins / what-each-wire-does / location / Operational Theori sit dimmed below; `missingLogic` stays hidden until NOW falls out of range, at which point "0V when commanded? → relay, fuse, pump driver" unfolds. LOCATE step (if the step is "find the lift pump"): lead with the location string ("driver-side frame rail") and SUPPRESS readings entirely — no EXPECT/NOW band, because a number is noise when the job is "find the physical thing." ORIENT step (entering the fuel sub-circuit): the circuit is named and framed, no pin elevated, no reading shown. BRANCH step (after an out-of-range reading resolves): lead with the verdict and the ONE next part lighting up (the relay/fuse upstream per `missingLogic`); alternatives the system didn't pick are never shown (no "step N of M", no upcoming-work preview — the system computes next silently). The ordering is a pure function of step type over the same loaded data; no extra fetch, instant on tap.
provenanceTreatment: Provenance reads as a quiet inline mono caption directly under the value it grades, never a badge wall — and The Meter has a single privileged place for it: the line directly beneath the EXPECT/NOW reading, because that is the number the tech is about to act on. The existing label map carries straight through: TRAINING-INFERRED → "inferred from theory" (the lift-pump pins are TRAINING-CONFIRMED in the data → "from theory", neutral graphite `--vt-fg-3`, no color — it's the default state, not an alarm), FIELD-VERIFIED → "field-verified" (a single quiet `--vt-signal-500` navy tick), GAP → "needs field check" (amber `--vt-amber-500`). Amber is the ONE provenance state allowed elevated salience and only at the moment of a probe — grounded in the tech's existing dash mental model (ISO 2575 / SAE J578: amber = advisory/caution, "verify before trusting", NOT red = measured-wrong). Red (`--vt-risk-high` / `--vt-risk-destructive`) is reserved strictly for an actual out-of-range/fault reading (the fault-low scenario's "flat low while cranking") so the tech's eye learns three states cleanly: graphite = drafted, amber = we-don't-know-yet, red = measured-wrong. Crucially, NO standing provenance pill rides on the node faceplate — the current `topo-node__gap` "needs field check" chip is removed; source grade instead modulates how the part is drawn (a field-verified part at full ink, an inferred part one shade lighter, a GAP part with a hairline-dashed edge — the universal schematic "provisional" convention). Confidence is felt before a word is read. The `pin.labelGap` text ("wire color, connector pin number, prime duration — not yet captured") lives only inside the expanded see-source path, never on the canvas.
scenarioStateTreatment: Scenario state and the live "now showing" reading live WITHIN the canvas and stay out of the way. Desktop: the active scenario is named INSIDE the instrument block's NOW column ("NOW (Key On · Engine Off)") so the live reading is self-labeling — the tech never has to cross-reference a separate strip to know which truck-state the number belongs to. The full scenario picker (the 4-group Ignition/Engine/Load + Pretend-fault controls, reusing `deriveCompound`/`findOperationSlug` unchanged) collapses into a single recessed chip in the top ribbon ("Key On · Engine Off ▾") that opens a compact scenario sheet on demand — it is NOT a persistent horizontal bar competing for room. The standalone "Now showing · …" strip from the current screen is deleted on desktop (its job is absorbed into the NOW column) and survives on mobile only as a thin status line pinned just above the sheet. Because the reading IS the hero, scenario changes feel like turning the key: flip the chip to "Engine Idle" and the lift-pump 12V NOW value settles from "12V on prime, then 0V" to "12V steady" with a damped ~280ms tnum transition (no jitter — tabular numerals), the FRP signal anchor shifts faintly, and the EXPECT band stays fixed as the reference. The curator-only captured/missing data-status (`topology.dataStatus`) is role-gated server-side and lives behind a curator-only entry in the ribbon — never the bottom overlay it is today, never shown to techs or owners.
seeSourcePath: The provenance caption word itself is the affordance — same pull-not-push grammar as the part detail. Under the reading sits "from theory · why?" (or amber "needs field check · why?"). Tapping it expands the why-we-believe-this INLINE, in place, pushing the secondary facts down briefly, then collapses on a second tap or background-tap — no standing "sources" column, no navigation away. The expanded content is short and every-word-counts: the source grade in plain terms ("Drafted from how this system works on the 6.7 Power Stroke — not yet verified against a wiring diagram for this exact truck"), plus the specific `pin.labelGap` unknowns when present ("Wire color, connector pin number, prime duration, and the upstream relay/fuse IDs aren't captured yet"). It never becomes a wall of architecture-facts theory rows (that would re-cross into the rejected push pattern). On mobile it's a ≥44px tap target that expands within the sheet's HALF detent. This is the literal expression of "carries evidence, never an oracle": the path always exists, stays silent until asked, answers in place, and gets out of the way.
visualLanguage: Adopt the in-repo "Workshop Instrument" tokens wholesale — the canvas should be indistinguishable in palette/type/density from the curator desktop kit, not a parallel system. TYPE: every reading and number in `--vt-font-mono` with `font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums` (the kit's `.vt-mono-data` / `.vt-mono-big`) so the EXPECT/NOW digits never jitter on scenario change — this single discipline is most of the gauge-vs-webtext feel; part names + the one-line Operational Theori in `--vt-font-serif` (Instrument Serif, editorial voice); Inter Tight micro-caps for the ribbon eyebrow only. COLOR: bone surface ramp (`--vt-bg`/`--vt-bone-50` through `--vt-fg`/`--vt-bone-900`); single navy accent `--vt-signal-500` for selection/focus halo + field-verified tick; `--vt-amber-500` reserved for "needs field check"; `--vt-risk-high`/`--vt-risk-destructive` reserved for out-of-range readings only. ADD (highest-leverage, confirmed missing): the six `--role-*` wire tokens are referenced across topology.css but defined NOWHERE, so every wire currently renders colorless — define them in globals.css `:root` on the element ramp: `--role-12v` → warm red near `--vt-risk-high` (power = hot), `--role-ground` → `--vt-fg-2` graphite (return = neutral), `--role-signal` → `--vt-elem-voltage` chartreuse, `--role-5v-ref` → `--vt-elem-coolant` cyan, `--role-low-ref` → desaturated coolant, `--role-pwm` → `--vt-elem-vacuum` violet. ADD `--vt-recede: 0.16` (the value already in `.wire.off`) as the canonical fade token for nodes/wires/labels. DENSITY/LINE: replace react-flow's default dotted `<Background />` and default `<Controls />` (the literal rejected default-AI look) — use the kit's near-invisible OKLCH graph-paper grid (`.vt-app` background, reads as bond paper) and a custom minimal "show whole system" control. NODE: replace the heavy 1.4px-bordered `.topo-node` card with an engraved faceplate — 0.5px hairline (every vt divider is 0.5px), serif name, mono micro-cap kind/location, pins the only high-contrast geometry; selection = navy ink halo + `--vt-accent-bg` tint, NOT the current 2px amber border (which collides with amber=caution). RADII small (`--vt-radius-1`..`-2`, "instruments don't have pillows").
appWidePropagation: Once the canvas proves the instrument language, it standardizes three things app-wide. (1) The reading-against-a-band instrument block becomes the canonical way Vyntechs shows any measured-vs-expected value — the same EXPECT/NOW/verdict component drops into the guided wizard's per-step reading capture, the curator's data-review surfaces, and any future scope/PID readout, always mono+tnum, always with the provenance caption beneath. (2) The provenance-as-ink + inline "why?" pull becomes the universal trust grammar: every drafted value anywhere in the product reads "from theory / needs field check / field-verified" as a quiet caption whose word is the see-source link, killing the badge-wall instinct everywhere (the current `topo-node__gap` chip dies here and nowhere else gains one). (3) The recede-by-relevance + fade-not-vanish mechanic, plus the six now-defined `--role-*` wire tokens and `--vt-recede`, graduate from canvas-only into shared tokens any electrical/diagram surface reuses — so the wiring REFERENCE view and the guided wizard share one visual contract instead of drifting. The desktop vt kit gains its first real mobile companion CSS here (the kit is desktop-only today), and that mobile layer — collapsed scenario chip, bottom-sheet detents, ≥48px touch targets — becomes the template for every future mobile-in-the-bay surface.
motionSpec: Calm, damped, no bounce — every motion does diagnostic work or is cut (the OP-1 test), all on the kit's `--vt-ease-instrument` (cubic-bezier(.4,0,.2,1)). KEY TRANSITIONS: (1) Step-change reframe — gentle `fitView` over `--vt-dur-4` (480ms) `interpolate:'smooth'`, run together with the opacity cross-fade of receded nodes/wires to `--vt-recede` over `--vt-dur-3` (280ms) so the eye reads one settle; the camera move is deliberately modest (maxZoom 1.1) because the reading rising is the louder beat. (2) The Meter rising from the bottom edge — translateY+opacity over `--vt-dur-3` (280ms) ease-out, no overshoot; dismiss reverses it. (3) Reading value change on scenario flip — the tnum digits settle (opacity/position micro-transition over `--vt-dur-2`, 160ms), never snap, like a damped needle (analog-gauge convention). (4) Focus-on-tap commit — start on pointerdown not click-resolve (all data is in-memory via the useMemo'd Maps, zero round-trip), a single decisive navy halo + ~1.02 scale on the tapped pin over `--vt-dur-1` (80ms), Things-3-confident not web-hover-soft. (5) Failure-tree unfold (only when out-of-range) — height+opacity reveal in place over `--vt-dur-3`. REDUCED-MOTION (hard branch, first-class): under `prefers-reduced-motion: reduce`, set all `fitView` durations to 0 (instant frame to the circuit, the most nausea-prone move dropped), keep ONLY opacity cross-fades for fade/focus, freeze wire dash-flow (the existing `@media` guard already does this for `.wire`) — the destination framing and the reading are identical, proving the felt value lives in the information design, not the animation.
citations: PicoScope 7 Automotive Guided Tests — fixed reference waveform compared against captured (expected-vs-actual by perception, not memory): https://www.picoauto.com/library/automotive-guided-tests/
PicoScope reference waveforms 'remain fixed on-screen' as the known-good band: https://www.picoauto.com/library/picoscope-automotive-training/knowledge-base/reference-waveforms

Snap-on ZEUS Guided Component Tests — WHERE to probe + connector/pinout + normal-operation context + live meter in the LOWER screen region: https://www.snapon.com/DiagnosticsManuals/ZEUS%20NA/Content/ZEUS/ComponentTest1.htm
Snap-on VERUS Edge — where-to-connect + what-result-to-expect, the trust bar a master tech holds the tool against: https://www.snapon.com/EN/US/Diagnostics/Products/VERUS-Edge
Expertise-reversal effect (Kalyuga/Sweller) — redundant guidance harms experts; defer the failure tree: https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/21443379/
iA Writer Focus Mode — dims everything except the current sentence (the active reading is that sentence): https://ia.net/writer/how-to/write-with-focus
Linear 2026 refresh — orientation chrome 'a few notches dimmer' so the work takes precedence; 'don't compete for attention you haven't earned': https://linear.app/now/behind-the-latest-design-refresh
KiCad net highlight — brighten the active net, dim everything else (fade-not-delete is the pro-EDA focus convention): https://docs.kicad.org/9.0/en/pcbnew/pcbnew.html

Altium Cross-Select Mode + persistent mask — keep focus stable during interactive work; re-resolve only on step change: https://www.altium.com/documentation/altium-designer/sch-pcb/cross-probing-selecting
React Flow fitView with nodes filter + per-side padding (the camera primitive already in the stack): https://reactflow.dev/api-reference/types/fit-view-options

Apple UISheetPresentationController detents + largestUndimmedDetentIdentifier — non-modal peek/half keeps the canvas live: https://developer.apple.com/documentation/uikit/uisheetpresentationcontroller/detent

Turo Engineering — shift the map camera as a bottom sheet moves so the point of interest stays visible above it: https://medium.com/turo-engineering/adjusting-compose-google-map-while-bottom-sheet-moves-4a7465305137

ISO 2575 / SAE J578 — amber=advisory/caution, red=stop-now; grounds provenance + fault color semantics in the tech's dash mental model: https://blog.ansi.org/ansi/sae-j-578-2020-color-lighting-motor-vehicles/
Material Design 3 bottom sheets — persistent vs modal; keep the background interactive at peek/half: https://m3.material.io/components/bottom-sheets/guidelines
NN/g Progressive Disclosure — contextual variant: surface extra info based on the user's current action (step-aware tap ordering): https://www.nngroup.com/articles/progressive-disclosure/
risks: The thesis demotes the spatial canvas, so it leans LEAST on the full-bleed 'diagram IS the workspace' spine — if the camera move is too small and the reading band too dominant, it can slide back into 'a widget with a readout.' Mitigation: the pin must always render lit on its REAL connector with the reading visibly anchored above the sheet (a hairline leader from pin to Meter), and the modest reframe must still visibly recede the harness so the diagram is unmistakably present, not decorative.

The bottom band on desktop eats vertical real estate the diagram could use; on a short laptop viewport the lit circuit + the Meter can crowd. Mitigation: the Meter is ~30% height max and the bottom-heavy fitView padding guarantees the lit pump sits in the upper band; the Meter eases away on background-tap to return full canvas.

Per-step-TYPE budget (locate/probe/interpret/branch) is more engine logic than a uniform highlight rule — more to build and more to get wrong; a mis-typed step shows the wrong leading fact (e.g. a reading on a LOCATE step). Mitigation: step type + focusPinId are authored data, not inferred; default to PROBE/INTERPRET only when a focusPinId exists, else ORIENT.

Reserving red strictly for out-of-range readings means the P0087 fault must be unambiguously detectable as out-of-range from `scenario.pinReadings` text (which today is prose like 'flat low while cranking', not a parsed number). If range-detection is fuzzy, the verdict chip and the failure-tree-unfold trigger become unreliable. Mitigation: ship the verdict as a curator-authored in-range/low flag per (pin,scenario) rather than parsing prose, or default to neutral (no red, tree stays collapsed) when ambiguous.

Glanceable single-reading focus can under-serve the moment a tech genuinely needs to compare two pins (e.g. lift-pump 12V AND ground together to split 'no power' from 'seized motor'). Mitigation: allow a second pin tap within the step to dock a compact second reading row beside the first rather than replacing it — but cap at two so it stays a meter, not a table.

Mobile: if the canvas-pan-up-on-sheet-open (Turo technique) is even slightly off, the sheet covers the lit pump — the exact self-defeating failure the spine forbids. Needs precise sheet-height-to-camera-offset coupling and testing at 375px specifically.

Amber 'needs field check' as the only elevated-salience provenance state risks habituation if many readings are gaps; if amber is everywhere it stops meaning 'verify.' Mitigation: amber only at the moment of a probe on the active reading, never standing on faded nodes.
buildNotes: Reality-check against the current @xyflow/react build (components/topology/*). NET-NEW: (1) a step model layered over the existing flat `SelectionState` — `{ focusPinId, circuitNodeIds, anchorNodeIds, stepType }` per step; the existing in-memory `componentById`/`pinById`/`connectionById` Maps already give zero-fetch tap data, so this is pure client state. (2) Drive the EXISTING `.wire.is-active`/`.wire.off`/`.wire.dim` classes from the step instead of pin-selection (already wired in `toFlowElements`/`topology-flow`), and extend `TopologyNode` to take a `tier` prop → class. (3) Replace `<Background />` + `<Controls />` with the kit grid + a custom 'show whole system' button that calls `fitView()` with no nodes filter. (4) Per-step `fitView({ nodes: circuitNodeIds.map(id=>({id})), padding:{...}, maxZoom:1.1, duration: reducedMotion?0:480 })` on step-change only — wrap `useReactFlow()` inside the provider. (5) Define the six `--role-*` tokens + `--vt-recede` in globals.css (one-line fix that makes wires render in color for the first time). REPLACE: the right-hand `<aside>` TopologyDetailPanel becomes the bottom-anchored Meter (desktop bottom band / mobile detented sheet) — the existing `PinBody` already computes `reading = scenario.pinReadings[pin.id]`, `pin.expectedReading`, `pin.missingLogic`; reorder it to lead with the instrument block, dim the secondary fields, and gate `missingLogic` behind an out-of-range flag. RE-USE: `deriveCompound`/`findOperationSlug` from scenario-bar.tsx unchanged behind the collapsed scenario chip; the `@media (prefers-reduced-motion)` guard already in topology.css; the `PROVENANCE_LABEL` map already in topology-detail-panel.tsx. MOBILE: net-new bottom-sheet detent component (peek/half/full, non-modal until full) + the canvas-pan-on-sheet-move coupling — the kit is desktop-only so this mobile CSS layer is genuinely new. RANGE FLAG: the riskiest dependency — needs a per-(pin,scenario) in-range/out-of-range boolean to trigger red + the failure-tree unfold reliably; recommend authoring it as curator data rather than parsing the prose `reading` strings. EFFORT: medium — most primitives (fitView, the wire focus classes, the data shape, the provenance map) already exist; the work is wiring the step model, the bottom-anchored Meter, the role-token definitions, and the mobile sheet, not new infrastructure.

check.direction: The Meter — the measured reading rendered as a gauge face (EXPECT vs NOW vs verdict) is the hero; the lift-pump circuit is the lit backdrop that proves WHERE the number comes from. Detail rises from the bottom edge as a canvas-internal sheet on tap.

spineCompliance.diagramIsWorkspace: PARTIAL — and this is the direction's own admitted weakest spine adherence (its risk #1). It deliberately demotes the spatial canvas in favor of the reading band: the camera move is a gentle settle (maxZoom 1.1), and a ~30% bottom band is given to the Meter. The full-bleed diagram still gets the majority of pixels and the receded harness stays drawn, so it does NOT violate the spine — but it leans on it least of the three siblings. The mitigation (pin must always render lit on its REAL connector with a hairline leader to the Meter; the reframe must visibly recede the harness) is credible and keeps it on the right side of the line. Watch item, not a fail.
spineCompliance.stepAwareOnlyWhatsNeeded: STRONG. Focus is organized around the active READING (focusPinId) + step circuitNodeIds, with a per-step-TYPE budget (locate/probe/interpret/branch) that genuinely differs by moment: a LOCATE step SUPPRESSES the reading entirely ("a number is noise when the job is find the physical thing"), ORIENT shows no pin elevated, PROBE leads with probeLocation then EXPECT/NOW. This is the most rigorous reading of only-what's-needed-now of the three — it gates not just which parts show but which FACT leads, and even suppresses the hero element when the step doesn't want it. Correctly grounds the lift-pump circuit (relay, pump, 12V, ground, fuse) as focus and FRP sensor as the single 'why we're here' anchor at 0.45, everything else receded.
spineCompliance.fadeNotVanish: STRONG and technically grounded. Tier model (focus/anchor/recede) drives a class, never display:none; CP4.2, all 8 injectors, FRP regulator, IMV, PCM stay drawn. Reuses the existing .wire.off (0.16, animation:none) and enforces a non-zero opacity floor so receded never becomes vanished. Names the real --vt-recede:0.16 value (correctly noting it's the value already baked into .wire.off and proposing to promote it to a named token — verified: .wire.off uses opacity:0.16 and --vt-recede is currently undefined). Fully compliant.
spineCompliance.tapToPullStepAware: STRONG. A tap is explicitly framed as the tech declaring intent; the Meter rises only on tap, eases down on background-tap, and is NOT a standing panel. WHICH fact leads is step-aware (probeLocation+EXPECT/NOW on a PROBE step; location-only on LOCATE). missingLogic (the failure tree) stays COLLAPSED on an in-range reading and unfolds only when out-of-range — a clean expression of pull-not-push and expertise-reversal. One caution: the desktop Meter occupies a 'fixed edge with a predictable home' bottom band ~30% — because it has a persistent home location it edges toward feeling like a docked surface. It is saved from violation by being empty/absent until a tap and easing away on dismiss (the canvas returns to full). This is pulled, not pushed — but it is the closest-to-the-line of the tap surfaces and depends on the dismiss actually returning full canvas as specified.
spineCompliance.contextWithinCanvas: STRONG. Scenario state is folded INTO the instrument block's NOW column ("NOW (Key On · Engine Off)") so the reading self-labels; the standalone 'Now showing' strip is deleted on desktop (absorbed) and survives only as a thin mobile status line. The full scenario picker collapses to a single recessed ribbon chip that opens a sheet on demand — not a persistent horizontal bar. The Meter is bottom-anchored and the asymmetric bottom-heavy fitView padding keeps the lit pump ABOVE where the sheet lands, satisfying 'never sits on top of the part the tech is working on.' All context lives within the canvas.
spineCompliance.curatorOnlyCompleteness: STRONG and explicit. The captured/missing dataStatus is role-gated SERVER-SIDE behind a curator-only ribbon entry, never the bottom overlay it is today, never shown to techs or owners. It also correctly proposes deleting the per-node topo-node__gap 'needs field check' chip (verified to exist at topology-node.tsx:64-65) and replacing standing-completeness signals with how-the-part-is-drawn. Fully compliant.

guardrailCompliance.noAiWord: PASS. I scanned every field of the direction's copy. No 'AI' word appears anywhere in user-facing strings. Provenance is framed around the source/action ('inferred from theory', 'needs field check', 'field-verified', 'Drafted from how this system works on the 6.7 Power Stroke — not yet verified against a wiring diagram'). The word 'drafted' is used as a state descriptor, never 'AI'. Clean.
guardrailCompliance.noStepNofM: PASS. Explicitly honored: BRANCH step 'leads with the verdict and the ONE next part lighting up; alternatives the system didn't pick are never shown (no step N of M, no upcoming-work preview — the system computes next silently).' The failure tree only unfolds on an actual wrong reading, never as a preview. No counted-progress leak anywhere.
guardrailCompliance.mobilePasses: PASS with real degradation, not a hand-wave. 44px ribbon, lift pump auto-framed into the TOP ~45% filling width, >=48px pin targets, a non-modal PEEK sheet (~150px, NO scrim, canvas stays live above it) with peek/half/full detents, reading as the largest type on the phone (mono tnum), provenance as a >=44px tap target. The Turo bottom-sheet camera-pan-on-move technique is correctly cited to keep the lit pump above the sheet. The direction HONESTLY flags (risk #6) that if the canvas-pan-to-sheet-height coupling is even slightly off, the sheet covers the lit pump — the exact spine-forbidden failure — and demands 375px-specific testing. That is the right risk to name. Existing CSS confirms a mobile baseline already exists (min 32px pin floor today; the direction raises it to >=48px). Credible, gated, not aspirational.
guardrailCompliance.provenanceHonest: PASS — and notably strong. Provenance is CORE, not decoration: a quiet inline mono caption directly beneath the value it grades, the caption WORD itself ('from theory · why?' / 'needs field check · why?') is the see-source affordance (pull-not-push, expands inline then folds away). The existing PROVENANCE_LABEL map is used verbatim (verified at topology-detail-panel.tsx:62-67: TRAINING-INFERRED->'inferred from theory', GAP->'needs field verification' — note the direction abbreviates GAP to 'needs field check' in places, which matches the brief's amber label but differs slightly from the code's 'needs field verification' string; minor copy reconciliation, not dishonesty). Three-state color discipline (graphite=drafted, amber=we-don't-know-yet, red=measured-wrong) is grounded in ISO 2575/SAE J578. The pin.labelGap unknowns live only inside the expanded see-source path, never on the canvas. The tool 'carries evidence, never an oracle' is the literal thesis of the seeSourcePath. Fully honest.
guardrailCompliance.notDefaultAiAesthetic: PASS. Explicitly replaces react-flow's default dotted <Background/> and default <Controls/> (named as 'the literal rejected default-AI look') with the kit's OKLCH graph-paper grid (verified to exist in v2.css:25-36 and v2-instruments.css) and a custom minimal control. Replaces the heavy 1.4px node border (verified at topology.css:91) with a 0.5px engraved hairline faceplate. No verdict pills (the single in-range/low chip is a meter verdict tied to a reading, NOT a decorative status pill), no emoji icon cards, no uniform-Tailwind spacing (uses the vt --vt-space/--vt-radius ramp). Cites real pro-tool conventions (PicoScope reference-waveform-fixed-on-screen, Snap-on ZEUS lower-screen live meter, KiCad net highlight, Altium persistent mask) with URLs. One real concern: the bone+navy+amber palette, Instrument Serif names, and mono-tnum readings are SHARED with both siblings — distinctness from the default-AI look is strong, but it is not visually differentiated FROM its own siblings (see distinctness).

check.distinctnessVsOthers: GENUINELY DISTINCT in thesis, but the distinction is conceptual more than visual. The Drive = turn-by-turn navigation through a circuit (camera flies, aggressive zoom). The Dimmer = the harness never moves, only the light changes (pure opacity, no camera). The Meter = the READING is the hero, the diagram is its backdrop (gentle settle camera, bottom instrument band is the loud beat). The Meter is the only one of the three whose organizing unit is the value-against-a-band rather than the region or the light, and the per-step-TYPE fact budget (LOCATE suppresses the reading entirely) is a mechanic neither sibling has — that is a real, defensible difference. RISK OF COLLAPSE: (1) The Meter explicitly says its camera does a 'gentle settle, never the aggressive zoom The Drive would use' AND that its fade reuses the same .wire.off mechanic The Dimmer is built around. So if the reading band is dialed down, The Meter's canvas behavior becomes nearly identical to The Dimmer (modest-to-no camera + opacity fade). The thing keeping it distinct is the bottom instrument band's prominence — which is also its spine-weakness. (2) All three share the exact same visual language (bone ramp, navy accent, amber caution, mono-tnum, Instrument Serif, the --role-* wire tokens, --vt-recede). That is correct per the brief (one visual contract) but means a reviewer comparing screenshots side-by-side will distinguish them by LAYOUT (where the reading sits, how much the camera moves), not by look. The Meter earns its slot on the reading-as-hero thesis + the step-type fact budget; it does not earn it on a unique aesthetic.
honestRisks: Spine-1 tension: this is the direction that leans LEAST on 'the diagram IS the workspace.' If the reading band is too dominant or the reframe too subtle, it slides toward 'a widget with a readout' — the exact current-state failure the brief is trying to escape. The whole bet rides on the bottom band staying <=30% and easing away, and on the hairline leader making the diagram feel load-bearing rather than decorative. Unproven until built.

The verdict/red/failure-tree-unfold trigger has NO structured data to stand on today. Verified: pinReadings is Record<string,string> (prose like '12V briefly during prime, then 0V'), expectedReading is a string, and the only structured fault signal is scenario.kind='operation'|'fault' — which is per-SCENARIO (whole diagram), not per-(pin,scenario). So 'red reserved for out-of-range' and 'tree unfolds only when wrong' require NEW curator-authored per-(pin,scenario) in-range booleans, or they degrade to coarse scenario.kind detection. The direction names this as its #1 build dependency and recommends authoring the flag — honest, but it means the headline behavior is NOT shippable on current data without a data change the brief says is out of scope ('do not change the loader contract / SystemDataDraft shape'). This is the single biggest gap between the pitch and what the code can do today.

Minor build inaccuracy: the direction says it reuses deriveCompound/findOperationSlug 'unchanged' behind the collapsed scenario chip — but verified these are INTERNAL functions in scenario-bar.tsx (not exported). Collapsing the picker into a ribbon chip + on-demand sheet requires either exporting them or rebuilding the picker. Small, but it undercuts the 'most primitives already exist, effort: medium' claim slightly.

Copy reconciliation: the direction uses 'needs field check' for GAP in several places, but the shipped PROVENANCE_LABEL map renders GAP as 'needs field verification' (verified topology-detail-panel.tsx:66). The brief's label map lists GAP->'needs field verification' too. 'needs field check' matches the brief's amber-state phrasing but not the code string — needs to be settled to one wording.

The two-pin-compare affordance (risk #5 mitigation: dock a second reading row, cap at 2) is sensible but adds a second interaction model on top of the single-reading thesis — it is the one place where 'the reading is the hero (singular)' starts to become 'a small table,' which is the thing the name is built against. Capping at 2 is the right guard but it's a seam.

Mobile camera-pan-to-sheet-height coupling (Turo technique) is the highest-precision unproven piece — if off by a little at 375px the sheet covers the lit pump, the spine-forbidden failure. Correctly flagged but genuinely fiddly; needs real device testing, not emulator.
suggestedFixes: Strengthen the spine-1 defense concretely: specify a MINIMUM camera recede (e.g. receded harness must drop to <=0.16 AND the focus circuit must occupy >=X% of the canvas after reframe) so 'the diagram is unmistakably present' is a measurable gate, not a hope. Otherwise this direction's whole risk is unbounded.

Resolve the verdict-data dependency before building, not during: either (a) commit to authoring a per-(pin,scenario) in-range/out-of-range boolean as curator data (and confirm with Brandon that this is allowed given 'don't change the loader contract'), or (b) default the verdict to NEUTRAL (no red, tree stays collapsed) whenever range is ambiguous, and ship the in-range chip + failure-tree-unfold as a curator-data enhancement layered on later. Pick one explicitly so the headline behavior isn't built on prose-parsing.
Fix the build note: deriveCompound/findOperationSlug are not exported — either export them or note the picker is rebuilt. Don't claim 'unchanged' reuse.
Settle GAP wording to one string ('needs field verification' per the shipped map and the brief's label map) so the amber caption matches the code; don't ship two phrasings.

Spec the dismiss-returns-full-canvas behavior as a hard requirement (Meter eases down AND camera does NOT move on dismiss is stated — confirm the full canvas is reclaimed visually, since the persistent bottom 'home' is what flirts with the docked-panel line).

Pin down the mobile sheet-height-to-camera-offset coupling with an explicit formula and require 375px device testing as the acceptance gate (the direction asks for this — make it a checklist item, not prose).
